THE COURT FURTHER ORDERS PETITIONERS TO SUBMIT FOR FILING A NEW PUBLIC VERSION OF THE BERGER DECLARATION THAT OMITS EXHIBIT 6 OR STATES THAT IT IS SEALED PURSUANT TO THIS COURT'S ORDER WITHIN FIVE COURT DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) ORDER IS SIGNED. . JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Court Order Regarding Nonparty Compliance With Subpoena For Production Of Documents (Ccp 2031.310(I))
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Off Calendar

PETITIONERS JOHNNY MURRAY, BENJAMIN CASTRO, AND RYLEE LIZCANO'S MOTION BY PETITIONERS TO COMPEL NONPARTY TO COMPLY WITH SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (ADDED FOR POSTING OF PART 2 OF 2 OF THE TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Petitioners To Compel Nonparty To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONERS JOHNNY MURRAY, BENJAMIN CASTRO, AND RYLEE LIZCANO's Motion To Compel Nonparty To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents.

The court grants Petitioners' unopposed request to compel further production of documents in response to subpoena by third party Episcopal Community Services of San Francisco. The Berger Declaration establishes that ECS represented it would produce documents responsive to RFPs 7-9 of Petitioners' subpoena. After repeated representations that further documents were forthcoming, ECS has not produced further documents and has equivocated about whether further responsive documents exist. (Berger Dec. paras. 19-28.)

The court orders ECS to produce documents responsive to RFPs 7-9, consistent with its representations of production in meet and confer correspondence, within 25 days of entry of this order. If ECS has no further responsive documents, it shall provide a verification so attesting. Petitioners do not seek sanctions, and the court awards none here.

Independent of the merits of this motion, the court notes that Petitioners have placed into the public Register of Actions an exhibit containing individual reasonable accommodation requests by shelter residents. (See Berger Dec. Ex. 6.) Some of the requests disclose confidential or sensitive medical and mental health information. The exhibit is unnecessary to the court's disposition of this motion and risks invasion of privacy of shelter residents, who are not parties to this case and have not placed their private information at issue. The court admonishes Petitioners' counsel to be mindful of the private information they receive in discovery from third parties in this case, and to consider with care whether that information needs to be filed with the court.

With respect to this issue, the court orders as follows. The court orders the clerk to seal the Berger Declaration. The court finds that there exists an overriding interest in protecting the medical and mental health privacy rights of shelter residents as described herein that overcomes the right of public access to this declaration; that this overriding interest supports sealing the Berger Declaration and replacing it with a new version of the Berger Declaration, as described in the next paragraph; that a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; that sealing the current version of the Berger declaration and replacing it with a new version as directed in the following paragraph is narrowly tailored; and that no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest in protecting the medical and mental health privacy of shelter residents.

The court further orders Petitioners to submit for filing a new public version of the Berger Declaration that omits Exhibit 6 or states that it is sealed pursuant to this court's order within five court days of entry of this order.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
